body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Ecology/Animism** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Nature connectedness — biophilia hypothesis, nature neuroscience, environmental neuroscience.

**A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis of EEG, fMRI, and fNIRS Studies on the Psychological Impact of Nature on Well-Being.**. Int J Environ Res Public Health.. PMID: 41899754. DOI: 10.3390/ijerph23030377. Exposure to nature has been associated with benefits to human well-being, commonly evaluated using standardized psychological assessments and, more recently, neuroimaging modalities such as Electroencephalography (EEG), functional Magnetic Resonance Imaging (fMRI), and functional Near-Infrared Spectroscopy (fNIRS).
